# Implementation Plan: Phase 1 - Data Discovery & Analytics

**Branch**: `002-data-discovery-analytics` | **Date**: 2025-10-22 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-data-discovery-analytics/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow for Phase 1 implementation.

## Summary

**Primary Requirement**: Build Analytics Dashboard, Intelligent Search & Discovery, and AI-Powered Feed Recommendations to enable data-driven decision making and content discovery for the AIWebFeeds platform.

**Technical Approach**: 
- **100% Free & Open-Source Stack**: SQLite with FTS5 (full-text search) + JSON1 (analytics storage) + triggers (materialized views)
- **Vector Search**: NumPy + SQLite BLOB for semantic embeddings (simple, zero dependencies) with optional upgrade path to sqlite-vec extension
- **ML/AI**: Local Sentence-Transformers (default) with optional Hugging Face Inference API for zero-compute embedding generation
- **Caching**: Python functools.lru_cache + SQLite TEMP tables (no Redis required)
- **Visualization**: Chart.js/Apache ECharts for interactive dashboards
- **Content-Based Recommendations**: Topic similarity + popularity (collaborative filtering deferred to Phase 2)

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.13+ (backend, data processing, ML) | TypeScript 5.9+ (web frontend, Next.js 15)  
**Primary Dependencies**: 
- **Backend**: SQLModel, Sentence-Transformers, scikit-learn, httpx, feedparser, tenacity, numpy, pydantic-settings, tqdm, loguru
- **Frontend**: Next.js 15, React 19, TailwindCSS 4, Chart.js or Apache ECharts
- **Optional**: Hugging Face Inference API (free tier, 1000 req/day)

**Storage**: SQLite 3.40+ with FTS5 (full-text search), JSON1 (analytics snapshots), WAL mode (concurrent reads), SQLModel ORM  
**Testing**: pytest (unit, integration, E2E, property-based with Hypothesis), ≥90% coverage required  
**Target Platform**: Cross-platform (Linux, macOS, Windows servers), responsive web UI (desktop/mobile browsers)  
**Project Type**: Hybrid monorepo (Python backend + TypeScript web app)  

**Performance Goals**:  
- Analytics dashboard: <2s page load (4G connection), 95% of queries cached
- Search autocomplete: <200ms response time (95th percentile)
- Full-text search: <500ms for 10,000+ feed catalog
- Semantic search: <3s total latency (2s vector search + 1s rendering)
- Recommendation generation: <1s (precomputed matrices)
- Support 1000 concurrent users without degradation

**Constraints**:  
- 100% free & open-source dependencies (no paid services)
- No external search engines (Meilisearch, Typesense, Elasticsearch) - SQLite FTS5 only
- No Redis - Python functools.lru_cache + SQLite TEMP tables for caching
- Optional HF API for embeddings (free tier: 1000 req/day), fallback to local model
- WAL mode for SQLite to support 1000+ concurrent readers
- Single-file SQLite database (<2GB for MVP scope)

**Scale/Scope**:  
- 1,000-50,000 feeds (SQLite FTS5 validated for 100K+ rows)
- 100-1,000 users initially (Phase 1 MVP)
- 10-20 recommendations per user per page load
- 3 user stories (Analytics, Search, Recommendations)
- 42 functional requirements
- No user accounts in Phase 1 (localStorage for personalization)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| **I. Documentation-First** | ✅ PASS | Analytics, search, and recommendations features will be documented in `apps/web/content/docs/features/*.mdx` with proper frontmatter and navigation updates. No standalone `.md` files will be created. |
| **II. Component Isolation** | ✅ PASS | Analytics logic in `packages/ai_web_feeds/`, CLI commands in `apps/cli/`, web pages/API in `apps/web/`. Clear boundaries maintained. |
| **III. Type Safety** | ✅ PASS | All Python code uses type hints (mypy strict), TypeScript strict mode, Pydantic models for data validation, SQLModel for database models. |
| **IV. Test-First** | ✅ PASS | ≥90% coverage required. Unit tests mirror source tree, mock external boundaries (HF API, SQLite in unit tests), integration tests exercise real SQLite queries, E2E tests verify user workflows. |
| **V. Data Schema Compliance** | ✅ PASS | New analytics entities (AnalyticsSnapshot, FeedEmbedding, etc.) will use SQLModel models with proper constraints. JSON data stored in SQLite JSON1 columns validated via Pydantic. |
| **VI. Modern Stack** | ✅ PASS | Python 3.13+, Next.js 15+, React 19+, Pydantic v2, SQLModel 0.0.14+. All dependencies pinned in `uv.lock` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`. |
| **VII. Code Quality** | ✅ PASS | Ruff linting, ESLint 9, conventional commits, absolute imports only, comprehensive error handling with Loguru, Google-style docstrings. |

**Pre-Design Status**: ✅ 7/7 principles satisfied (100% compliance)

**Post-Design Status**: ✅ 7/7 principles satisfied (100% compliance)

**Post-Design Verification**:
- ✅ **Documentation-First**: All design docs created as `.md` within `specs/` directory (correct location for design artifacts)
- ✅ **Component Isolation**: Modules properly separated (analytics, search, recommendations, embeddings in `packages/`)
- ✅ **Type Safety**: SQLModel models defined, Pydantic Settings configured, OpenAPI spec generated
- ✅ **Test-First**: Test structure defined in quickstart, ≥90% coverage requirement maintained
- ✅ **Data Schema Compliance**: JSON schemas created for AnalyticsSnapshot, SearchQuery, SavedSearch
- ✅ **Modern Stack**: All dependencies match project requirements (Python 3.13+, Next.js 15+)
- ✅ **Code Quality**: Absolute imports specified, error handling patterns documented, conventional commits required

**Phase 1 Design Artifacts Generated**:
1. ✅ `research.md` - Technology decisions and rationale (10 sections, all free/open-source)
2. ✅ `data-model.md` - Complete data model (8 new entities, SQLModel schemas, migrations)
3. ✅ `contracts/openapi.yaml` - REST API specification (19 endpoints, 3 categories)
4. ✅ `contracts/schemas/*.json` - JSON schemas (AnalyticsSnapshot, SearchQuery, SavedSearch)
5. ✅ `quickstart.md` - Development workflow and verification guide
6. ✅ Agent context updated (`.cursor/rules/specify-rules.mdc`)

**Constitution Compliance**: ✅ PASS (Ready for `/speckit.tasks` phase)

```text

packages/ai_web_feeds/              # Core Python package
├── src/ai_web_feeds/

│   ├── search.py                   # NEW: Full-text & semantic search
│   ├── recommendations.py          # NEW: Content-based filtering

│   ├── config.py                   # EXTEND: Add embedding settings
│   └── (existing: load, validate, enrich, export, logger, utils)

└── uv.lock

apps/cli/                           # Typer CLI interface
├── ai_web_feeds/cli/commands/
│   ├── analytics.py                # NEW: Analytics dashboard commands
│   ├── search.py                   # NEW: Search CLI commands
│   ├── recommendations.py          # NEW: Recommendation generation CLI
│   └── (existing: load, validate, enrich, export)
└── pyproject.toml

apps/web/                           # Next.js 15 documentation site
├── app/
│   ├── analytics/
│   │   └── page.tsx                # NEW: Analytics dashboard page
│   ├── search/
│   │   └── page.tsx                # NEW: Search interface page
│   ├── recommendations/
│   │   └── page.tsx                # NEW: Recommendations page
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── analytics/
│   │   │   └── route.ts            # NEW: Analytics data API
│   │   ├── search/
│   │   │   └── route.ts            # NEW: Search API (existing)
│   │   └── recommendations/
│   │       └── route.ts            # NEW: Recommendations API
│   └── (existing: docs, feeds, exports, stats)
├── components/
│   ├── analytics/                  # NEW: Chart components
│   ├── search/                     # NEW: Search UI components
│   └── recommendations/            # NEW: Recommendation cards
├── content/docs/
│   ├── features/
│   │   ├── analytics.mdx           # NEW: Analytics feature docs
│   │   ├── search.mdx              # NEW: Search feature docs
│   │   └── recommendations.mdx     # NEW: Recommendations feature docs
│   └── meta.json                   # UPDATE: Add new pages to nav
├── package.json
└── pnpm-lock.yaml

data/                               # Data files (YAML, JSON, SQLite)
├── aiwebfeeds.db                   # EXTEND: Add analytics tables
├── feeds.yaml
├── topics.yaml
└── *.schema.json

tests/                              # Pytest test suite
├── tests/packages/ai_web_feeds/
│   ├── test_analytics.py           # NEW: Analytics unit tests
│   ├── test_search.py              # NEW: Search unit tests
│   ├── test_recommendations.py     # NEW: Recommendations unit tests
│   ├── test_embeddings.py          # NEW: Embeddings unit tests
│   └── (existing: test_load, test_validate, etc.)
└── pyproject.toml
```

**Structure Decision**: 
- **Backend logic** (analytics calculations, search, recommendations, embeddings) implemented in `packages/ai_web_feeds/src/ai_web_feeds/` as pure Python modules
- **CLI orchestration** added to `apps/cli/ai_web_feeds/cli/commands/` for command-line access
- **Web UI** implemented in `apps/web/` with Next.js pages, API routes, and React components
- **Documentation** added to `apps/web/content/docs/features/*.mdx` (not standalone `.md` files)
- **Tests** added to `tests/tests/packages/ai_web_feeds/` mirroring source structure
- **Database schema** extended in existing `data/aiwebfeeds.db` via SQLModel migrations

## Complexity Tracking

✅ **No Constitution Violations** - All 7 principles satisfied (100% pre-design compliance)

**Complexity Justifications** (design decisions):

| Design Choice | Rationale | Alternative Considered |
|---------------|-----------|------------------------|
| **SQLite over PostgreSQL** | Single-file, zero-config, serverless, 100% open-source (public domain). Handles 1000+ concurrent reads with WAL mode. Sufficient for MVP (1K-50K feeds, 100-1K users). | PostgreSQL (rejected): Requires server setup, heavier infrastructure, overkill for MVP scale. |
| **NumPy BLOB embeddings over sqlite-vec** | Zero external dependencies, simple implementation, good enough for <10K feeds. Upgrade path to sqlite-vec if performance becomes bottleneck. | sqlite-vec extension (deferred): Faster but adds dependency, can be added later without migration. |
| **functools.lru_cache over Redis** | Python stdlib, no server required, sufficient for autocomplete and trending topics (5-30min TTL). Simpler architecture. | Redis (rejected): External server, additional complexity, unnecessary for MVP caching needs. |
| **Local Sentence-Transformers over HF API** | Default option is zero-setup, no rate limits, no API token required. HF API is optional for users who want to offload compute. | Cloud-only embeddings (rejected): Would lock users into external dependency, against OSS principles. |
| **Content-based only (defer collaborative filtering)** | No user accounts in Phase 1, so no user-user interaction data. Content-based (topic similarity) provides value without accounts. | Implement collab filtering now (rejected): Requires user accounts system (Phase 2), premature for MVP. |
